name: Volere Requirements Specification Template (Robertson & Robertson, Ed. 16 / Mastering the Requirements Process 4e)
summary: The Volere requirement-type taxonomy: one functional type plus a rich set of non-functional types and project constraints, each with a fit criterion. Used here to map real-world requirement categories to coverage domains, especially the non-functional and constraint areas ISO models cover only loosely.

leaves.name: Functional requirements
leaves.description: The things the product must do — the fundamental subject matter, business rules and processing actions.

leaves.name: Look and feel requirements
leaves.description: The intended appearance and style — branding, mood, visual design constraints (Volere type 10).

leaves.name: Usability and humanity requirements
leaves.description: Ease of use, personalization, learning, understandability/politeness, and accessibility for special user groups (Volere type 11).

leaves.name: Performance requirements
leaves.description: Speed, precision, capacity, throughput, reliability and safety-of-scale expectations (Volere type 12).

leaves.name: Operational and environmental requirements
leaves.description: The operating environment, expected physical/technological conditions, and partner/interfacing systems (Volere type 13).

leaves.name: Maintainability and support requirements
leaves.description: How easily the product can be maintained, supported and ported, and expected release/support conditions (Volere type 14).

leaves.name: Security requirements
leaves.description: Access, confidentiality/integrity, privacy, and audit expectations for the product (Volere type 15).

leaves.name: Cultural and political requirements
leaves.description: Human and social factors — organizational politics, cultural expectations, and localization sensitivities (Volere type 16).

leaves.name: Legal / compliance requirements
leaves.description: Laws, regulations and standards the product must comply with (Volere type 17).

leaves.name: Project constraints
leaves.description: Global constraints on the solution — budget, schedule, mandated business rules and design limits (Volere project constraints).

leaves.name: Mandated / off-the-shelf solutions
leaves.description: Mandated technologies, off-the-shelf products, or partner solutions the product must use or fit within.
